title: Fruits & Vegetables
description: Master Slovak fruits and vegetables at the market

rows.label: Potato
rows.english: Potatoes (plural — almost always bought in bulk; zemiakový šalát = potato salad, a Slovak party staple)

rows.label: Carrot
rows.english: Carrot — eaten raw, in soups and stews. Mrkva is the base of many Slovak broths alongside cibuľa and cesnak

rows.label: Onion
rows.english: Onion — a backbone of Slovak cooking. Goes into practically every savoury dish: guláš, kapustnica, polievky (soups)

rows.label: Garlic
rows.english: Garlic — used heavily in Slovak cooking. Cesnak + cibuľa = the flavour base of most Slovak hot dishes

rows.label: Pepper
rows.english: Pepper / Sweet pepper — also refers to the ground red spice. Paprika (the vegetable) is eaten raw or stuffed (plnená paprika)

rows.label: Tomato
rows.english: Tomato — used in salads, sauces and stews. In Slovak markets you weigh loose paradajky yourself at the váha

rows.label: Cucumber
rows.english: Cucumber — eaten fresh in salads or pickled (nakladaná uhorka = pickled cucumber). Very popular as a snack

rows.label: Lettuce
rows.english: Lettuce / Salad leaves — šalát means both the vegetable and the prepared salad dish

rows.label: Cabbage
rows.english: Cabbage — central to Slovak cuisine. Sauerkraut (kyslá kapusta) is the key ingredient in kapustnica soup

rows.label: Broccoli
rows.english: Broccoli — widely available in Slovak supermarkets; also used in cream soups (brokolicová polievka)

rows.label: Apple
rows.english: Apple — Slovak orchards produce excellent apples. Jablkový závin (apple strudel) is a beloved dessert

rows.label: Pear
rows.english: Pear — also means 'light bulb' in Slovak (same word)! Eaten fresh or in jams (lekvár)

rows.label: Banana
rows.english: Banana — high in potassium. One of the most bought fruits in Slovak supermarkets year-round

rows.label: Orange
rows.english: Orange — popular for vitamin C. Pomarančový džús = orange juice. Widely sold at trhnice (market stalls)

rows.label: Strawberry
rows.english: Strawberry — a summer favourite. Slovak june markets overflow with fresh jahody at great prices

rows.label: Cherry
rows.english: Cherry — dark sweet čerešne are a summer treat. Slovak cherry season is June–July

rows.label: Plum
rows.english: Plum — gives its name to the famous Slovak spirit slivovica (plum brandy). Abundant in village gardens

rows.label: Peach
rows.english: Peach — grown in warmer Slovak regions. Best bought at summer trhnica (market) stalls

rows.label: Grapes
rows.english: Grapes — Slovakia has a long winemaking tradition. Vinič (grape vine) grows across the south and east

rows.label: Lemon
rows.english: Lemon — used for lemonade, in teas and cooking. Rich in vitamin C. Citrusové ovocie = citrus fruit

scenarios.situation: You want to make the traditional Slovak Christmas soup kapustnica. Which vegetable is the key ingredient?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Kapustnica is made from kapusta (sauerkraut / fermented cabbage) — hence the name! The soup also contains klobása (sausage), smoked meat, mushrooms and spices. It's a staple at Slovak Christmas celebrations.

scenarios.situation: Slovakia's famous plum brandy is called slivovica. Which fruit gives it its name?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Slivka = plum. Slivovica (plum brandy) takes its name directly from slivka! The -ovica suffix turns a fruit into a spirit. Slovakia produces excellent slivovica, especially in village homesteads in rural areas.

scenarios.situation: At the summer market you see a sign: 'Čerstvé jahody — 3€/kg'. What is being sold?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Jahoda = strawberry. Čerstvé = fresh. /kg = per kilogram. Slovak summer markets overflow with seasonal jahody in June — along with čerešne (cherries) and broskyňe (peaches) at very good prices.

scenarios.situation: You want to squeeze fresh juice rich in vitamin C. Which fruit do you pick?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Citrón = lemon — packed with vitamin C. Pomaranč (orange) is also excellent for vitamin C. Both are available year-round in Slovak supermarkets. Lemonade made from fresh citróny is a popular summer drink.

scenarios.situation: Which of these four is zelenina (a vegetable), not ovocie (a fruit)?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Paprika = pepper — botanically a fruit, but used and categorised as zelenina (vegetable) in Slovak cooking! Banán, Hruška (pear) and Pomaranč (orange) are all ovocie (fruit). Slovak paprika can be sweet (sladká) or hot (pálivá).

scenarios.situation: Your recipe for a traditional Slovak dish says 'pridajte cibuľu a cesnak'. What do you need?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Cibuľa = onion, Cesnak = garlic. These two are the flavour backbone of Slovak cooking — you'll find them in guláš, kapustnica, and almost every savoury dish. Pridajte = add (imperative, plural/formal).

scenarios.situation: Which fruit is most associated with Slovak winemaking traditions?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Hrozno = grapes — the basis of wine (víno). Slovakia has established wine regions around Nitra, Bratislava, and the Tokaj area in the east. Look out for Slovenské víno (Slovak wine) on restaurant menus — often excellent value!

scenarios.situation: You pick up loose tomatoes at the supermarket. Before going to the till, what must you do?
choices: Put them in the fridge section
Weigh them at the váha and print a barcode sticker
scenarios.answer: Weigh them at the váha and print a barcode sticker
scenarios.answerMeaning: Loose paradajky and other zelenina must be weighed at the self-service váha (scale) in the produce section. Print the barcode sticker and stick it on the bag before going to the pokladňa (checkout). Forgetting to do this is a common beginner mistake!

items.sentence: Dám si ___ a jahody — milujem čerstvé letné ovocie.
items.translation: I'll have ___ and strawberries — I love fresh summer fruit.

items.sentence: Potrebujem ___ na šalát — sú červené, šťavnaté a zdravé.
items.translation: I need ___ for the salad — they are red, juicy and healthy.

items.sentence: Kúp mi ___, prosím — chcem urobiť domácu limonádu.
items.translation: Please buy me ___ — I want to make homemade lemonade.

items.sentence: Do guláša vždy dávam ___ a mrkvu — to je základ.
items.translation: I always put ___ and carrots into the goulash — that's the base.

items.sentence: ___ sú základ pre výrobu slivovice — tradičného slovenského destilátu.
items.translation: ___ are the basis for making slivovica — a traditional Slovak spirit.

items.sentence: Potrebujem ___ do šalátu — je to chrumkavá zelená zelenina.
items.translation: I need ___ for the salad — it's a crunchy green vegetable.

items.sentence: Chcem si kúpiť ___ — je to žlté ovocie bohaté na draslík.
items.translation: I want to buy ___ — it's a yellow fruit rich in potassium.

items.sentence: Na kapustovú polievku potrebujem ___ a bravčové mäso.
items.translation: For the cabbage soup I need ___ and pork.

items.sentence: Dám si ___ a jahody — milujem čerstvé letné ovocie.
Potrebujem ___ na šalát — sú červené, šťavnaté a zdravé.
Kúp mi ___, prosím — chcem urobiť domácu limonádu.
Do guláša vždy dávam ___ a mrkvu — to je základ.

exercises.scenario: 🛍️ You are at an outdoor market (trhnica) in Bratislava buying fresh produce.

turns.snailMessage: Dobrý deň! Čo si prajete? Máme čerstvú zeleninu aj ovocie.
turns.snailMessageEnglish: Good day! What would you like? We have fresh vegetables and fruit.

choices.english: Please, how much are the tomatoes?

turns.snailReply: Paradajky sú dve eurá za kilogram. Čerstvé z farmy!
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Tomatoes are two euros per kilogram. Fresh from the farm!

choices.english: I'll have a kilogram of tomatoes please.

turns.snailReply: Výborne! Chcete aj nejakú inú zeleninu?
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Excellent! Would you like any other vegetables?

choices.slovak: Áno, dajte mi aj kapustu a mrkvu, prosím.
choices.english: Yes, please give me cabbage and carrots too.

turns.snailReply: Dobre! Kapusta je euro päťdesiat, mrkva osemdesiat centov za kilogram.
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Good! Cabbage is one euro fifty, carrots are eighty cents per kilogram.

choices.slovak: Dám si pol kilogramu kapusty a kilogram mrkvy.
choices.english: I'll have half a kilogram of cabbage and one kilogram of carrots.
I don't want cabbage or carrots.

turns.snailReply: Samozrejme! A máme aj skvelé letné ovocie — jahody, broskyňe aj hrozno.
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Of course! And we also have great summer fruit — strawberries, peaches and grapes.

choices.slovak: Výborne! Dám si jahody a broskyňe, prosím.
choices.english: Excellent! I'll have strawberries and peaches please.

turns.snailReply: Jahody sú tri eurá za kilogram, broskyňe dve eurá päťdesiat.
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Strawberries are three euros per kilogram, peaches two euros fifty.

choices.slovak: Dám si pol kilogramu jahôd a pol kilogramu broskyňí.
choices.english: I'll have half a kilogram of strawberries and half a kilogram of peaches.

turns.snailReply: Dobre! Ešte niečo? Máme aj čerstvý cesnak a brokolicu.
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Good! Anything else? We also have fresh garlic and broccoli.

choices.slovak: Nie, ďakujem, to je všetko. Koľko platím spolu?
choices.english: No thank you, that's all. How much is it altogether?

turns.snailReply: Spolu je to osem eur dvadsať. Platíte kartou alebo hotovosťou?
turns.snailReplyEnglish: The total is eight euros twenty. Are you paying by card or cash?

choices.slovak: Hotovosťou, prosím. Tu je desať eur.
choices.english: By cash please. Here is ten euros.
I have neither cash nor card.

turns.snailReply: Ďakujem! Váš zvyšok je jedno euro osemdesiat. Príjemný deň a dobrú chuť!
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Thank you! Your change is one euro eighty. Have a lovely day and enjoy your food!
